title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Language Lens: Transactional Outbox Record
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must compose database transaction ownership, relay confirms, duplicate-safe inbox effects, timeouts, and compensation state; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transactional outbox record increment in a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model. Learners progr…
objectives: Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Transactional Outbox Record · Theory Transactional Outbox Record
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 9508 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; reject stale connection and channel generations; then reuse a message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
